questions.question: Define 'binary' in the context of binary trees. Provide an example to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: A binary tree is a tree in which each node has at most two children. For instance, the following diagram represents a binary tree:

questions.question: What is the difference between a left child and a right child in a binary tree? Provide an example to support your answer.
questions.hint: In a binary tree, the left child refers to the node that is located to the left of the parent node, while the right child refers to the node that is located to the right of the parent node.

questions.question: Explain the concept of a root node in a binary tree. Provide an example to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: In a binary tree, the root node is the topmost node that is not a leaf node. It is called the root because it serves as the foundation or starting point for the tree.
